questions.question: AUTHOR : NOVEL ::
questions.explanation: An AUTHOR creates a NOVEL. A COMPOSER creates a SYMPHONY. Creator to creation.

wrongExplanations.0: A painter USES a brush — tool, not creation.
wrongExplanations.2: An actor PERFORMS ON a stage — location, not creation.
wrongExplanations.3: A chef WORKS IN a kitchen — location, not creation.

questions.question: WHISPER : SHOUT ::
questions.explanation: WHISPER is quiet speaking; SHOUT is loud. DRIZZLE is light rain; DOWNPOUR is heavy. Same intensity relationship.

wrongExplanations.0: Walking IS moving — category, not intensity.
wrongExplanations.2: Speak and talk mean the same thing.
wrongExplanations.3: Read and write are different actions.

questions.question: TELESCOPE : ASTRONOMER ::
questions.explanation: A TELESCOPE is the tool an ASTRONOMER uses. STETHOSCOPE is the tool a DOCTOR uses.

wrongExplanations.0: Hammer hits a nail — tool to object.
wrongExplanations.2: Book is in a library — item to location.
wrongExplanations.3: Shoe is worn by a runner — clothing, not special tool.

questions.question: FAMISHED : HUNGRY ::
questions.explanation: FAMISHED is extreme HUNGRY. FURIOUS is extreme ANNOYED. Strong to mild.

wrongExplanations.0: Tired and sleepy are similar — not strong vs. mild.
wrongExplanations.2: Happy and glad mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: FREEZING is the extreme of COLD — order is flipped.

questions.question: ISLAND : OCEAN ::
questions.explanation: ISLAND is surrounded by OCEAN. OASIS is surrounded by DESERT. Surrounded-by.

wrongExplanations.0: Mountain is next to a valley.
wrongExplanations.2: Bridge crosses a river.
wrongExplanations.3: Forest and meadow are different lands next to each other.

questions.question: CALF : COW ::
questions.explanation: CALF is a young COW. PUPPY is a young DOG. Young to adult.

wrongExplanations.1: Order reversed — kitten is young, cat is adult.
wrongExplanations.2: Bird lives in a nest — habitat.
wrongExplanations.3: Fish lives in water — habitat.

questions.question: CHAPTER : BOOK ::
questions.explanation: CHAPTER is a section of BOOK. VERSE is a section of POEM. Part to whole.

wrongExplanations.1: Letter is part of a word — backwards.
wrongExplanations.2: Cover is on the OUTSIDE of a magazine.
wrongExplanations.3: Shelf holds books — container.

questions.question: ANCIENT : MODERN ::
questions.explanation: ANCIENT and MODERN are opposites in time. TALL and SHORT are opposites in height.

wrongExplanations.1: Quick and fast mean the same.
wrongExplanations.2: Bright and shiny are similar.
wrongExplanations.3: Large and huge are degrees of the same.

questions.question: CAPTAIN : SHIP ::
questions.explanation: CAPTAIN commands SHIP. PILOT commands AIRPLANE. Person controlling vehicle.

wrongExplanations.1: Passenger RIDES a bus.
wrongExplanations.2: Wheel is part of a car — not a person.
wrongExplanations.3: Track is what a train runs on.

questions.question: COCOON : BUTTERFLY ::
questions.explanation: COCOON is the stage BEFORE BUTTERFLY. EGG is the stage BEFORE CHICKEN.

wrongExplanations.1: Cave is where a bat lives — habitat.
wrongExplanations.2: Shell is part of a turtle's body.
wrongExplanations.3: Web is what a spider builds — tool.

questions.question: MIGRATE : BIRD ::
questions.explanation: MIGRATE is a SEASONAL behavior of a BIRD. HIBERNATE is a SEASONAL behavior of a BEAR.

wrongExplanations.0: Fish swim all the time.
wrongExplanations.2: Dogs bark all the time.
wrongExplanations.3: Horses gallop whenever.

questions.question: RECIPE : CHEF ::
questions.explanation: RECIPE is a PLAN a CHEF follows. BLUEPRINT is a PLAN an ARCHITECT follows.

wrongExplanations.1: Paint is APPLIED to a wall.
wrongExplanations.2: Hammer is a TOOL — not a plan.
wrongExplanations.3: Song PLAYS on a radio.

questions.question: TIMID : BOLD ::
questions.explanation: TIMID and BOLD are opposites. CRUEL and KIND are opposites.

wrongExplanations.0: Quiet and silent mean the same.
wrongExplanations.1: Happy and cheerful mean the same.
wrongExplanations.2: Wet and damp are similar.

questions.question: SECONDS : MINUTE ::
questions.explanation: 60 SECONDS = 1 MINUTE. 12 INCHES = 1 FOOT. Smaller unit to bigger unit.

wrongExplanations.0: Days to year is similar but inches to foot is the cleaner measurement match.
wrongExplanations.2: Pages to chapter isn't a fixed measurement.
wrongExplanations.3: Backwards — hours are bigger than minutes.

questions.question: DOCTOR : HOSPITAL ::
questions.explanation: DOCTOR works in HOSPITAL. JUDGE works in COURTROOM. Person to workplace.

wrongExplanations.0: Student VISITS library to study.
wrongExplanations.2: Painter's work is DISPLAYED in museum.
wrongExplanations.3: Farmer SELLS at a market.

questions.question: SCALE : WEIGHT ::
questions.explanation: SCALE measures WEIGHT. RULER measures LENGTH. Tool to what it measures.

wrongExplanations.1: Thermometer measures temperature.
wrongExplanations.2: Clock has a hand — part to whole.
wrongExplanations.3: Map shows a country — doesn't measure.

questions.question: DROUGHT : RAIN ::
questions.explanation: DROUGHT is a LACK of RAIN. FAMINE is a LACK of FOOD.

wrongExplanations.0: Flood is TOO MUCH rain — opposite of lack.
wrongExplanations.2: Desert HAS sand.
wrongExplanations.3: Snow happens IN winter.

questions.question: NOVEL : FICTION ::
questions.explanation: NOVEL is a TYPE OF FICTION. BIOGRAPHY is a TYPE OF NONFICTION.

wrongExplanations.0: Poem may have rhyme but rhyme isn't a category.
wrongExplanations.2: Magazine CONTAINS articles.
wrongExplanations.3: Newspaper is PRINTED with ink — material.

questions.question: SEED : PLANT ::
questions.explanation: A SEED is what a PLANT grows from. A SPARK is what a FIRE grows from. Beginning to result.

wrongExplanations.0: Root is part of a tree.
wrongExplanations.1: A drop is part of an ocean — part to whole.
wrongExplanations.3: Key opens a lock — different relationship.

questions.question: GLOVE : HAND ::
questions.explanation: GLOVE is worn on HAND. SOCK is worn on FOOT. Clothing to body part.

wrongExplanations.1: Hat is on head, but glove specifically covers FINGERS like sock covers TOES.
wrongExplanations.2: Scarf wraps around neck — not the same fit.
wrongExplanations.3: Shoe goes on foot, not leg.

questions.question: DOCTOR : PATIENT ::
questions.explanation: DOCTOR helps PATIENT. TEACHER helps STUDENT. Person who helps to person being helped.

wrongExplanations.1: Writer uses a pen — tool relationship.
wrongExplanations.2: Store and customer is location/person — different.
wrongExplanations.3: Mother and father are family relationship.

questions.question: GROW : SHRINK ::
questions.explanation: GROW and SHRINK are opposites. CLIMB and DESCEND are opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: Walk and run are both moving — different speeds.
wrongExplanations.2: Laugh and smile are similar emotions.
wrongExplanations.3: Eat and drink are different actions, not opposites.

questions.question: BRICK : WALL ::
questions.explanation: BRICK is a part that builds a WALL. PAGE is a part that builds a BOOK. Building block to structure.

wrongExplanations.1: Door is one piece of a house — not a building block.
wrongExplanations.2: Window is made of glass — material relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Roof and ceiling are similar parts.

questions.question: MUSIC : COMPOSER ::
questions.explanation: COMPOSER creates MUSIC. AUTHOR creates STORY. Creator to creation.

wrongExplanations.1: Frame surrounds a painting.
wrongExplanations.2: Actor performs on stage.
wrongExplanations.3: Museum displays art.

questions.question: WET : SOAKED ::
questions.explanation: WET is mild, SOAKED is extreme. BIG is mild, HUGE is extreme. Mild to extreme.

wrongExplanations.0: Warm and hot — direction is right but BIG/HUGE is closer.
wrongExplanations.1: Tired and sleepy mean similar things.
wrongExplanations.3: Cold and freezing — also mild to extreme, but BIG/HUGE is more parallel.

questions.question: BAKER : BREAD ::
questions.explanation: BAKER makes BREAD. CARPENTER makes a HOUSE. Worker to product.

wrongExplanations.0: Farmer uses a tractor — tool, not product.
wrongExplanations.2: Artist's work is shown in a museum — location.
wrongExplanations.3: Sewing is the activity, not the product.

questions.question: INHALE : EXHALE ::
questions.explanation: INHALE and EXHALE are opposites of breathing. ACCEPT and REJECT are opposites of receiving.

wrongExplanations.0: Sit and stand are different positions but not the same kind of opposite (not push/pull).
wrongExplanations.2: Jump and leap mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: Sing and hum are similar.

questions.question: BICYCLE : ROAD ::
questions.explanation: BICYCLE travels on ROAD. BOAT travels on WATER. Vehicle to surface it travels on.

wrongExplanations.1: Station is where a train stops, not what it travels on.
wrongExplanations.2: Driver controls a car — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Airport is where a plane takes off, not what it travels on.

questions.question: PICK : POCKET ::
questions.explanation: PICK + POCKET means to steal from. KICK + BALL — these aren't the same kind of analogy. Wait — let's reread: VERB + NOUN, with action applied to the object. PICK pocket = action on pocket. KICK ball = action on ball. Both are simple verb-object.

wrongExplanations.0: Wash + car is similar but kick:ball is more parallel as a quick action.
wrongExplanations.2: Fill + glass — also similar, but pick:pocket is most familiar phrase.
wrongExplanations.3: Cut + grass is also action on object.

questions.question: BARK : DOG ::
questions.explanation: BARK is the sound a DOG makes. MEOW is the sound a CAT makes.

wrongExplanations.1: Feather is part of a bird — not a sound.
wrongExplanations.2: Fin is part of a fish.
wrongExplanations.3: Leaf is part of a tree.

questions.question: FORGET : REMEMBER ::
questions.explanation: FORGET and REMEMBER are opposites. LOSE and FIND are opposites.

wrongExplanations.0: Leave and depart mean the same.
wrongExplanations.2: Sleep and dream go together but aren't opposites.
wrongExplanations.3: Drop and fall are similar actions.

questions.question: GRAPE : VINE ::
questions.explanation: GRAPE grows on VINE. APPLE grows on TREE. Fruit to where it grows.

wrongExplanations.1: Tomatoes grow on a vine, not the ground.
wrongExplanations.2: Wheat grows in a field but is a grain — different.
wrongExplanations.3: Lettuce grows in a garden — too general.

questions.question: COURAGE : COWARDICE ::
questions.explanation: COURAGE and COWARDICE are opposite traits. KINDNESS and CRUELTY are opposite traits.

wrongExplanations.0: Honesty and truth are similar.
wrongExplanations.2: Wisdom and knowledge are similar.
wrongExplanations.3: Strength and power are similar.

questions.question: CARROT : ORANGE ::
questions.explanation: CARROT is typically ORANGE. BANANA is typically YELLOW. Item to its color.

wrongExplanations.1: Apple is also typically red, but BANANA/YELLOW is more universally true.
wrongExplanations.2: Tomato is food — too general.
wrongExplanations.3: Potato can be many colors — not a strong match.

questions.question: PRAISE : CRITICIZE ::
questions.explanation: PRAISE and CRITICIZE are opposites. ATTACK and DEFEND are opposites.

wrongExplanations.0: Help and assist mean the same.
wrongExplanations.1: Build and create are similar.
wrongExplanations.3: Walk and stroll are similar.

questions.question: BEE : HIVE ::
questions.explanation: BEE lives in HIVE. BIRD lives in NEST. Animal to its specific built home.

wrongExplanations.0: Fish lives in water — habitat, not built structure.
wrongExplanations.2: Wolves don't live in forests as a structure.
wrongExplanations.3: Pasture is where cows graze, not a built home.

questions.question: CHEF : KITCHEN ::
questions.explanation: CHEF works in KITCHEN. MECHANIC works in GARAGE. Worker to workplace.

wrongExplanations.1: Fan visits a stadium — not workplace.
wrongExplanations.2: Audience is who an actor performs FOR, not workplace.
wrongExplanations.3: Passenger needs a ticket — different relationship.

questions.question: ARTIST : BRUSH ::
questions.explanation: ARTIST uses BRUSH. WRITER uses PEN. Worker to their primary handheld tool.

wrongExplanations.0: Oven is bigger equipment, not a handheld tool.
wrongExplanations.2: Cloth is the material, not the tool.
wrongExplanations.3: Wood is material, not tool.

questions.question: SLOW : FAST ::
questions.explanation: SLOW and FAST are opposites. NEW and OLD are opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: Good and best are degrees of same.
wrongExplanations.2: High and tall are similar.
wrongExplanations.3: Quiet and silent are similar.

questions.question: SCHOOL : STUDENT ::
questions.explanation: SCHOOL is where STUDENT goes to learn. HOSPITAL is where PATIENT goes to be treated. Place to person served.

wrongExplanations.1: Product is what's sold, not who goes there.
wrongExplanations.2: Book is the item, not the person.
wrongExplanations.3: Driver controls a car — different relationship.

questions.question: PEN : INK ::
questions.explanation: PEN uses INK to write. PENCIL uses LEAD to write. Tool to the marking material it uses.

wrongExplanations.1: Wheel is part of a car.
wrongExplanations.2: Page is part of a book.
wrongExplanations.3: Smoke comes from fire — result, not writing material.

questions.question: INSECT : SPIDER ::
questions.explanation: INSECTS and SPIDERS are commonly confused but are different categories (spiders are arachnids). FISH and WHALES are commonly confused but are different categories (whales are mammals).

wrongExplanations.0: Dogs and cats are both mammals — same category.
wrongExplanations.1: Milk comes from cows — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Flowers can be on a tree but the relationship is part-of.

questions.question: OBVIOUS : HIDDEN ::
questions.explanation: OBVIOUS (visible) and HIDDEN (concealed) are opposites. PUBLIC (open) and PRIVATE (closed) are opposites.

wrongExplanations.0: Bright and shiny mean similar things.
wrongExplanations.1: Huge and enormous mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: Fast and quick mean the same.

questions.question: SAW : CARPENTER ::
questions.explanation: SAW is the cutting tool of a CARPENTER. NEEDLE is the cutting/sewing tool of a TAILOR. Tool to user.

wrongExplanations.1: Wool comes from sheep — material from animal.
wrongExplanations.2: Fork is used to eat food — wrong relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Tree is in a forest — part of.

questions.question: SUN : DAY ::
questions.explanation: SUN is the main feature of DAY. MOON is the main feature of NIGHT. Heavenly body to its time.

wrongExplanations.1: Stars are in the sky — part of, not main feature of a time.
wrongExplanations.2: Cloud causes rain — cause to effect.
wrongExplanations.3: Wind can cause a storm — cause to effect.

questions.question: TRIP : JOURNEY ::
questions.explanation: TRIP and JOURNEY are similar (a journey is just a longer trip). WALK and HIKE are similar (a hike is just a longer walk).

wrongExplanations.1: Yell and whisper are opposites in volume.
wrongExplanations.2: Sleep is longer than nap, but the order matters — short to long, not opposite.
wrongExplanations.3: Run and jog are different speeds, but the order is reversed (jog is slower).

questions.question: SCISSORS : CUT ::
questions.explanation: SCISSORS are used to CUT. PEN is used to WRITE. Tool to its action.

wrongExplanations.1: Cup HOLDS what you drink — you drink from it.
wrongExplanations.2: Plate HOLDS food — doesn't perform eating.
wrongExplanations.3: Mirror lets you see yourself — different relationship.

questions.question: GIANT : DWARF ::
questions.explanation: GIANT (very big) and DWARF (very small) are opposites of size. LARGE and SMALL are opposites of size.

wrongExplanations.0: Tall and tower — a tower is tall, not opposite.
wrongExplanations.2: Old and ancient mean similar things.
wrongExplanations.3: Quiet and whisper are similar.

questions.question: PUDDLE : LAKE ::
questions.explanation: PUDDLE (small water) and LAKE (big water). HILL (small) and MOUNTAIN (big). Small version to large version.

wrongExplanations.1: River is bigger than stream — order is reversed.
wrongExplanations.2: Tree is part of a forest — part to whole.
wrongExplanations.3: Cloud is in the sky — part of.

questions.question: FREEZE : MELT ::
questions.explanation: FREEZE (water → ice) and MELT (ice → water) are opposite changes of state. STOP and START are opposite actions.

wrongExplanations.0: Heat and cool are also opposite states, but stop:start more directly captures the action reversal.
wrongExplanations.1: Build and break are different actions, not exact reverses.
wrongExplanations.3: Push and pull are opposites of force, not state.

questions.question: BANK : MONEY ::
questions.explanation: BANK stores MONEY. LIBRARY stores BOOKS. Place to what it stores.

wrongExplanations.1: Store sells items — different.
wrongExplanations.2: Museum displays art.
wrongExplanations.3: Kitchen prepares food — doesn't store as primary purpose.

questions.question: WHEAT : FLOUR ::
questions.explanation: WHEAT is processed into FLOUR. MILK is processed into CHEESE. Raw material to processed product.

wrongExplanations.1: Leaf is part of a tree — not processed product.
wrongExplanations.2: Stone can be carved into statue — but more like raw to art.
wrongExplanations.3: Sand is part of a beach.

questions.question: FAMILIAR : STRANGE ::
questions.explanation: FAMILIAR and STRANGE are opposites. COMMON and RARE are opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: Old and ancient mean similar things.
wrongExplanations.2: Near and close mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: Good and great are degrees, not opposites.

questions.question: SMOKE : FIRE ::
questions.explanation: SMOKE comes from FIRE (effect from cause). SOUND comes from BELL. Effect to cause.

wrongExplanations.0: Rain comes from clouds — but it's a closer match for sound:bell.
wrongExplanations.1: Wind moves a tree — wind isn't FROM a tree.
wrongExplanations.3: Light comes from sun, not moon (moon reflects).

questions.question: SUBMARINE : OCEAN ::
questions.explanation: SUBMARINE travels through OCEAN. AIRPLANE travels through SKY. Vehicle to medium it travels in.

wrongExplanations.0: Bicycle is on road, not in.
wrongExplanations.2: Train follows track — on top of, not through.
wrongExplanations.3: Car is on highway.

questions.question: DEPOSIT : WITHDRAW ::
questions.explanation: DEPOSIT (put in) and WITHDRAW (take out) are opposites. ENTER (go in) and EXIT (go out) are opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: Arrive and leave are also opposites — but enter/exit is the cleaner same-pattern match.
wrongExplanations.2: Push and shove mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: Wait and stop aren't direct opposites.

questions.question: HONEYCOMB : BEE ::
questions.explanation: BEE builds HONEYCOMB. BIRD builds NEST. Animal to structure it builds.

wrongExplanations.1: Milk comes FROM a cow — product, not structure.
wrongExplanations.2: Pasture is where sheep graze — not built by them.
wrongExplanations.3: River is where fish live — not built.

questions.question: GENEROUS : STINGY ::
questions.explanation: GENEROUS and STINGY are opposite traits. KIND and MEAN are opposite traits.

wrongExplanations.0: Happy and joyful mean the same.
wrongExplanations.1: Brave and bold mean the same.
wrongExplanations.3: Tall and huge are degrees, not opposites.

questions.question: SCREEN : MOVIE ::
questions.explanation: MOVIE is shown on a SCREEN. PLAY is performed on a STAGE. Performance to its venue surface.

wrongExplanations.0: Story is contained IN a book.
wrongExplanations.2: Letter is on a page.
wrongExplanations.3: Painting is on a wall — but a wall isn't the performance space the way a stage is.

questions.question: RIVER : DELTA ::
questions.explanation: DELTA is where a RIVER meets the sea (an end-shape). INTERSECTION is where ROADS meet. Place where things converge.

wrongExplanations.1: Root is part of a tree.
wrongExplanations.2: "End" is too vague — delta is specifically the splitting end.
wrongExplanations.3: Peak is the TOP of a mountain — different shape relationship.

questions.question: PRIDE : LIONS ::
questions.explanation: PRIDE is a group of LIONS. PACK is a group of WOLVES. Specific group word to animal.

wrongExplanations.1: Flock applies to birds, not trees.
wrongExplanations.2: Herd is for cows/sheep, not people.
wrongExplanations.3: Swarm is for insects, not nests.

questions.question: FERTILE : BARREN ::
questions.explanation: FERTILE (full of life/growth) and BARREN (empty/no life) are opposites. FULL and EMPTY are opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: Dry and wet are opposites — but full/empty more closely captures the idea.
wrongExplanations.2: Hot and warm are degrees of same.
wrongExplanations.3: Large and big mean the same.

questions.question: BANANA : PEEL ::
questions.explanation: BANANA has a PEEL you remove. EGG has a SHELL you remove. Outer covering you discard.

wrongExplanations.1: Core is the INSIDE of an apple, not outer covering.
wrongExplanations.2: Seed is inside a grape.
wrongExplanations.3: Stem is the small handle, not the covering.

questions.question: SCREAM : SHRIEK ::
questions.explanation: SCREAM and SHRIEK both mean very loud cry — same intensity. RUN and SPRINT both mean fast running — same intensity.

wrongExplanations.0: Whisper and speak are different intensities.
wrongExplanations.1: Laugh and cry are opposite emotions.
wrongExplanations.3: Walk and hike are different intensities.

questions.question: PUPPY : LITTER ::
questions.explanation: PUPPIES are born together as a LITTER. BIRDS group together as a FLOCK. Group word for the species.

wrongExplanations.1: Forest is many trees but they aren't 'born together.'
wrongExplanations.2: School is a group of fish — but they aren't a family group like a litter is.
wrongExplanations.3: Family is a generic group — fits but flock is the specific match.

questions.question: PRESENT : PAST ::
questions.explanation: PRESENT (now) and PAST (before) are time opposites. TODAY (now) and YESTERDAY (before) are time opposites.

wrongExplanations.1: New and old are time-related but more general than past/present.
wrongExplanations.2: Start and finish are about action, not time period.
wrongExplanations.3: Now and later — later is FUTURE, not PAST.

questions.question: RICH : WEALTHY ::
questions.explanation: RICH and WEALTHY are synonyms. BRAVE and BOLD are synonyms.

wrongExplanations.0: Poor and sad are different — poor is about money, sad about emotion.
wrongExplanations.1: Large and huge are degrees (large to extreme).
wrongExplanations.2: Big and large mean similar things — also a valid match, but brave:bold is the cleanest synonym pair.

questions.question: COMPASS : DIRECTION ::
questions.explanation: COMPASS shows DIRECTION. CLOCK shows TIME. Tool to what it shows.

wrongExplanations.1: Map shows a country but is more general.
wrongExplanations.2: Mirror reflects a face — different relationship.
wrongExplanations.3: Window provides a view — passive, not measuring.
